item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The twist didn't quite work for me, as I feel it comes a bit too early. I think this would be structured by putting the intent of the ritual into the description, and then having an Addendum with the actual result (friendly pet) after the Exploration Log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dialogue is quite clunky, but article is well written and has an obvious amount of care put into it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Really liked the concept and the twist. This SCP is really interesting, great job :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I want one. Do you think the crazy cult guys take requests?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've read a few ritual based SCPs and some have been good. Others… Not so much. This one was rather good to read through personally and I liked how it didn't end up in something that wanted to murder everything in sight like a pseudo-682 but instead protects someone and makes their life generally better. It's a nice change.

+1 (Sadly can't +5)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually like this article, and it's going to get an upvote from me because I'd like to see it survive and grow but I think there are a few issues here and there that need fixing.

There are still places where the grammar could be improved upon. It's a bit too late where I am now to go back into it immediately, but I'm happy to do so in the near future.

There's mention of made of a "Germanic language" without further identification. Even if it's an extinct Germanic language, such as Gothic, I think the Foundation would be able to identify it. If it's something that is only identifiable as Germanic but doesn't have historical precedent, they might describe it as "heretofore unattested Germanic language," or something similar. I think the Foundation would either have done their research and identified it, or would make a statement to the effect of "it resembles this and we're still identifying it."

There are a few other things nagging me about the dialogue, but honestly I don't think I can articulate my gripes well enough to do you any good, so I'll refrain for now. If I think of anything more specific and constructive, I'll bring it up.

All in all, though, I like the bones on this thing, and want to see it continue.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page.

In this case, the image is licenced under a Non-Derivative licence, which is not compatable with the sites licence. The image will have to be removed or replaced with one that is compatable.

EDIT: Image has been removed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image of the monstrous whatsit shambling in, the agent maintaining kayfabe, and the dear old lady introducing 'Jonesey' as a beloved pet - it's like The Thing on the Fourble Board with a happy ending.

I wonder if any of the cultists have started wondering why they're getting cuddly animal friends instead of eldritch abominations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted, but was on the edge. Like the concept. I'd say some verbosity could be removed in the description, but I think the part that could be improved the most is the Event-1 transcript. Think it could just be replaced with a description of the events, especially the "monstrous creature just gently shoved the agent away and ran" part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Corrected the ones you pointed out, I'll sit and look more thoroughly later today. Thanks for feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed a spacing error in the Addendum 3624-A transcript.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's like they're trying to recreate a 628 and only seem to end up with wholesome pets instead.

Ones that even seem to actively seek out someone who 'needs' one.

It's a good concept :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea.
The speech needs a bit more coherence in its tone.
I'm giving a +1 to motivate you on going on with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept behind this one - it's actually pretty refreshing. A horrible ritual meant to create a hostile monster ends up creating a series of beneficial, albeit deformed pets. That said, there are a number of tone issues (particularly in the dialogue) that lead to my current downvote - I encourage you to keep editing this, however, because it's got potential.

+ Some examples
- Hide examples

It involves a number of requisites, most commonly being animal body parts, although on a single occasion a human arm was successfully utilized.

A human arm is an animal body part - scientifically speaking, there shouldn't be anything particularly special about it. It's also an odd sentence in general. You mention a number of requisites, then only specify animal body parts. I suggest altering the sentence to something along the lines of:

Each SCP-3624 ritual utilizes a selection of material components as a base, most frequently a variety of freshly severed animal body parts. No pattern has been observed in the components selected for each ritual; practitioners appear to be largely opportunistic. These body parts are then stitched together in a shape that roughly resembles a predatory animal (see Addendum 3624-01 for a breakdown of each observed attempt).

Though I'm not particularly satisfied with that either. I was staring at it for about half an hour before deciding a suggestion didn't necessarily need to be perfect.

"They are certainly nutters and they don't know we're here."

This dialogue is somewhat unnatural. "They are certainly" is formal, and it's followed immediately by an informal "nutters", which comes off as jarring at best. This would better fit the tone you're conveying with his words.

"They're nutters. No way they know we're here."

And so on. Unfortunately I don't have the time to be completely comprehensive, but I hope that selection helps, at least.

item.title: Posting
item.content:encoded:
If this won't be deleted, I'll post a thanks list.

Edit: Since this is still up and even in positives: big thanks to minmin, Mr Hat, DrBleep, Icepick, Atlach and others who helped me write this.
